destinations.evidence: The pool-aware EVM builder clones the shared BuilderContext SPC verifier, wraps it in SpcVerifierImpl, and stores it on the executor provider together with the block verifier.

equivalence_argument: The shared verifier is not recreated independently; it is cloned from BuilderContext and converted to the trait-object form used by RitualBlockExecutorProvider, preserving builder/executor SPC verifier identity for the verification path.
notes: Mapped in nodebuilder through the pool-aware RitualExecutorBuilder implementation.
rationale: The new method clones `ctx.spc_verifier()`, wraps it in `SpcVerifierImpl`, and passes it as `Some(spc_verifier)` together with the new block verifier. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: `build_evm_with_pool` uses the `BuilderContext` shared SPC verifier when constructing the executor provider, rather than creating an unrelated verifier instance.
suggested_falsification_tests: Configure a `BuilderContext` with a test SPC verifier, call `build_evm_with_pool`, and assert SPC verification during block execution uses that same configured verifier while also having block verification enabled.
summary: The pool-aware executor keeps the BuilderContext SPC verifier when adding block verification.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Configure BuilderContext with a distinctive SPC verifier, call build_evm_with_pool, and assert registry/SPC calls go through the cloned context verifier.
title: Pool-aware executor keeps shared SPC verifier
